description_long: This object is a long, straight sword with a double-edged blade that tapers to a pointed tip. The blade appears metallic and features subtle vertical striations, suggesting a forged or machined finish. The crossguard is flat and extends horizontally, providing hand protection, while the handle is cylindrical with a sculpted, ergonomic grip and a rounded pommel at the end. The handle and crossguard are both dark in color, possibly made of metal or a dark composite material. The overall design is utilitarian with no visible ornamentation, giving it a functional and robust appearance. The sword is proportioned for two-handed use, typical of medieval or fantasy longswords.
description: A long, double-edged straight sword with a dark, sculpted handle and a flat crossguard, featuring a pointed metallic blade and a robust, utilitarian design.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full length of the straight, double-edged blade, flat crossguard, and sculpted handle.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the thin profile of the blade and the cylindrical shape of the handle.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, displaying the blade, crossguard, and handle from the opposite side.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the thinness of the blade and the handle's profile.
attribute_handle_design: sculpted cylindrical handle, dark material, rounded pommel, ergonomic grip
